description: A designed informational poster on a dark navy textured (denim/canvas-like) background. The header reads 'ICE local and state law enforcement' in smaller white text above a large white serif title 'PARTNER PROGRAMS'. Down the left side are three stacked photo tiles tinted in red and blue duotone: the top tile (red) shows a jail/cell-block scene; the middle tile (blue) shows officers in tactical gear with 'POLICE' visible; the bottom tile (grayscale) shows a hand writing/signing a document. To the right of each tile is a bold white heading with description: 'Jail Enforcement Model — ID and process removable aliens you arrest with active or pending criminal charges.'; 'Task Force Model — Allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority in certain situations.'; 'Warrant Service Officer — Serve and carry out administrative warrants against illegal aliens.' Bottom left reads 'ICE.gov/287g' and bottom right shows the DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'. This is part of the 'FIRST 100 DAYS: Partnerships' series. The technique presents the 287(g) program in a clean, professional recruitment layout to encourage local/state agencies to partner with ICE. Target audience: police departments and sheriffs' offices.
notable_text: ICE local and state law enforcement PARTNER PROGRAMS; Jail Enforcement Model / Task Force Model / Warrant Service Officer; ICE.gov/287g
